trade.summary: Giappone is one of Indonesia's top trade partners. Indonesia exports LNG, coal, palm oil, minerals, and seafood to Giappone. Giappone exports automobiles, electronics, machinery, and steel. The IJEPA (Indonesia-Giappone Economic Partnership Agreement) has governed trade since 2008.

diplomatic.summary: Giappone-Indonesia relations are at a strategic partnership level, elevated in 2023. Giappone is among Indonesia's most important development partners, providing ODA for infrastructure. Historical ties go back to the post-WWII era. Cooperation is deep across trade, development, and regional security.

military.summary: Giappone provides maritime security assistance to Indonesia including patrol vessels via ODA and capacity building. Joint exercises in counter-piracy and humanitarian assistance. Giappone's defence posture expansion has increased defence dialogue with ASEAN partners including Indonesia.

financial.summary: Giappone is Indonesia's largest ODA provider and a leading source of FDI. Giapponeese companies are deeply embedded in Indonesian manufacturing (Toyota, Honda), infrastructure (MRT Jakarta), and energy. JBIC and JICA are active in Indonesian project finance.

energy.summary: Indonesia is a key LNG and coal supplier to Giappone. Giappone has invested heavily in Indonesian upstream energy. Cooperation on just energy transition (JETP) and hydrogen supply chains is growing.

scientific.summary: Strong academic and R&D ties. Giapponeese universities maintain active exchange programmes with Indonesian counterparts. JSPS and similar bodies fund joint research. Cooperation in disaster risk reduction is notable given both countries' seismic risk.
